questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
Given,

sin-1x+sin-1y=2π3   ...(i)

and cos-1x-cos-1y=π3   ...(ii)

We know that sin-1x+cos-1x=π2

Using the above concept, we can write 

⇒ π 2 - sin - 1 x - π 2 - sin - 1 y = π 3

⇒-sin-1x+sin-1y=π3   ...(iii) 

On adding equation i and iii, we get

sin - 1 y = π 2 ⇒ y = 1

On subtracting equation i from equation iii, we get

sin - 1 x = π 6 ⇒ x = 1 2

∴ x, y = 1 2 , 1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> in the expression <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>k</mi><mfenced open="[" close="]" separators="|"><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> is:
questions.solution:
We have, sin-1x+sin-1y+sin-1z=π

⇒sin-1x+sin-1y=π-sin-1z

⇒sin-1x1-y2+y1-x2=π-sin-1z

⇒x1-y2+y1-x2=z  ∵ sinπ-θ=sinθ

⇒x1-y2-z=-y1-x2

square both sides we get, 

⇒x21-y2+z2-2xz1-y2=y21-x2 

⇒x2+z2-y2=2xz1-y2

Again square both sides we get, 

⇒x4+z4+y2-2x2y2+2x2z2-2y2z2=4x2z2-4x2z2y2

⇒x4+z4+y4-4x2z2y2 =2x2y2+y2z2+z2x2

Compare then we get, k=2.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\cos ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{3}=\alpha$, where $-1 \leq x \leq 1$, $-3 \leq y \leq 3, x \leq \frac{y}{3}$, then for all $x, y$, $9 x^2-6 x y \cos \alpha+y^2$ is equal to
questions.solution:
If $\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}-\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{b}=\theta$, then $\frac{x^2}{\mathrm{a}^2}-\frac{2 x y}{\mathrm{ab}} \cos \theta+\frac{y^2}{\mathrm{~b}^2}=\sin ^2 \theta$ Given, $\cos ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{3}=\alpha$ Here, $\mathrm{a}=1, \mathrm{~b}=3$
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad & \frac{x^2}{1^2}-\frac{2 x y}{(1)(3)} \cos \alpha+\frac{y^2}{3^2}=\sin ^2 \alpha \\
& \Rightarrow x^2-\frac{2 x y}{3} \cos \alpha+\frac{y^2}{9}=\sin ^2 \alpha \\
& \Rightarrow 9 x^2-6 x y \cos \alpha+y^2=9 \sin ^2 \alpha
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution:
It is given that cos-1x22+1-x21-x24=cos-1x2-cos-1x

Since, 0≤cos-1⁡x22+1-x21-x24≤π2

Because cos-1⁡x is in first quadrant when x is positive

And cos-1 ⁡x2-cos-1⁡x≥0

So, cos-1 ⁡x2≥cos-1⁡x

Also, x2≤1⇒|x|≤1

π2-π6=sin-13x

3x=32

⇒ x=4

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Given $0 \leq x \leq \frac{1}{2}$, then the value of $\tan \left(\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{2}}+\frac{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)-\sin ^{-1} x\right)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \tan \left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{2}}+\frac{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)-\sin ^{-1} x\right] \\ & =\tan \left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+\sqrt{1-x^2}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)-\sin ^{-1} x\right] \\ & =\tan \left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin \theta+\cos \theta}{\sqrt{2}}\right)-\theta\right] \quad \ldots\left[\begin{array}{l}\text { Put } \sin ^{-1} x=\theta \\ \Rightarrow x=\sin \theta\end{array}\right] \\ & =\tan \left[\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right]-\theta\right] \\ & =\tan \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}-\theta\right) \\ & =\tan \frac{\pi}{4}=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mo>cosec</mo><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mo>·</mo></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer function. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> equals;
questions.solution:
Given,

fx=cosec-11+sin2x, where · denotes the greatest integer function.

Since, 

-1≤sinx≤1

⇒0≤sin2x≤1

⇒1≤1+sin2x≤2

⇒1+sin2x=1, 1≤sin2x<2 and

1+sin2x=2, if  2≤1+ sin2x<3.

So,

fx=cosec-11

⇒fx=π2

And,

fx=cosec-12

⇒fx=cosec-12

Therefore, fx has two values

π2,cosec-12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

So, both values give the same result. Therefore, it has only one  value to take.

1+cot2⁡A=1+x2=cosec2A

1 sin 2  A = 1 + x 2   ⇒   sin A = 1 / 1 + x 2

⇒ sin cot -1  x = 1 / 1 + x 2

questions.q: If the value of the expression <math><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow></math> is in the form of <math> <mrow> <mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt> <mi>b</mi> </msqrt> </mrow> </math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>Z</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow> <mi>b</mi> </mfrac> </mrow> </math> is K, Find 10K.
questions.solution:
We know that,

tan⁡x2=1-cos⁡xsin⁡x 

∴  tan⁡12cos-1⁡25 =1-cos⁡cos-1⁡25sin⁡cos-1⁡25

=1-2/5sin⁡sin-1⁡1/5

=1-2/51/5=5-2

Now,  a+ b =−2+ 5

⇒a=−2 and b=5

Hence,  a+b b = 3 5 =0.6

sin-1x-12≤0
⇒sin-1x-1=0⇒sin-1x=1
⇒1≤sin-1x<2 but, sin-1x∈-π2,π2
∴1≤sin-1x≤π2
⇒sin⁡1≤x≤sinπ2

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=\tan ^{-1}(\sec x+\tan x), \quad$ then $\frac{d y}{d x}=$
questions.solution:
Given 
$\begin{aligned} y &=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\cos x}+\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1+\sin x}{\cos x}\right) \\ &=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\left(\cos ^{2} \frac{x}{2}+\sin ^{2} \frac{x}{2}\right)+2 \sin \frac{x}{2} \cos \frac{x}{2}}{\cos ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-\sin ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}\right) \\ &=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{x}{\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}+\sin \frac{x}{2}\right)\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}-\sin \frac{x}{2}\right)}\right\} \\ &=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\cos \frac{x}{2}+\sin \frac{x}{2}}{\cos \frac{x}{2}-\sin \frac{x}{2}}\right\}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1+\tan \frac{x}{2}}{1-\tan \frac{x}{2}}\right) \\ &=\tan ^{-1}\left(\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{x}{2}\right)\right) \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{y} &=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{2} \\ \therefore \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}} &=0+\frac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{2} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
Here $\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x}{1+x}\right)=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{1-x}{1+(1)(x)}\right]=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x$
$\therefore \tan ^{-1}(1)-\tan ^{-1} x=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x$
$\frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{3}{2} \tan ^{-1} x \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{4} \times \frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{6}$
$\therefore x=\tan \frac{\pi}{6}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: With usual notations in $\Delta \mathrm{ABC}$, if $C=90^{\circ}$, then $\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a}{b+c}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{b}{c+a}\right)=$
questions.solution: $\therefore \quad \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a}{b+c}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{b}{c+a}\right)$ $=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{a}{b+c}+\frac{b}{c+a}}{1-\left(\frac{a}{b+c}\right)\left(\frac{b}{c+a}\right)}\right]$ $=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{a c+a^{2}+b c+b^{2}}{a c+b c+a b+c^{2}-a b}\right]=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a c+b c+c^{2}}{a c+b c+c^{2}}\right) \quad \ldots\left[\because a^{2}+b^{2}=c^{2}\right.$, refer diagram $]$ $=\tan ^{-1}(1)=\frac{\pi}{4}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & x=\operatorname{cosec}\left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(\cot ^{-1}\left(\sec \left(\sin ^{-1} \mathrm{a}\right)\right)\right)\right)\right) \\ & =\operatorname{cosec}\left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(\cot ^{-1}\left(\sec \left(\sec ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{a}^2}}\right)\right)\right)\right)\right) \\ & =\operatorname{cosec}\left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{a}^2}}\right)\right)\right)\right) \\ & =\operatorname{cosec}\left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{2-\mathrm{a}^2}}\right)\right)\right) \\ & =\operatorname{cosec}\left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2-\mathrm{a}^2}}\right)\right) \\ & =\operatorname{cosec}\left(\operatorname{cosec}^{-1}\left(\sqrt{3-\mathrm{a}^2}\right)\right) \\ \therefore \quad & x=\sqrt{3-\mathrm{a}^2} \\ \therefore \quad & x^2+\mathrm{a}^2=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The natural domain of the function <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac></msqrt></math> is
questions.solution:
Here, 2x∈-1,1 and sin-1⁡2x+π3≥0
Hence, x∈-12,12 and x≥-34
So the domain is -34,12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \cos ^{-1} \sqrt{\mathrm{p}}-\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{1-\mathrm{p}}+\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{1-\mathrm{q}}=\frac{3 \pi}{4} \\ & \text { Let } \mathrm{t}=\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{\mathrm{p}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{p}=\cos ^2 \mathrm{t} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{p}=1-\sin ^2 \mathrm{t} \\ & \Rightarrow \sin \mathrm{t}=\sqrt{1-\mathrm{p}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-\mathrm{p}}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \cos ^{-1} \sqrt{\mathrm{p}}=\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-\mathrm{p}}$
$\therefore \quad$ Given equation becomes
$\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-p}-\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{1-p}+\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{1-q}=\frac{3 \pi}{4}$
$\therefore \quad \frac{\pi}{2}+\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{1-q}=\frac{3 \pi}{4} \quad \ldots\left[\because \cos ^{-1} a+\sin ^{-1} a=\frac{\pi}{2}\right]$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & \cos ^{-1} \sqrt{1-q}=\frac{-\pi}{4} \\ \therefore & \sqrt{1-q}=\cos \left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ \therefore & q=1-\frac{1}{2} \\ \therefore & q=\frac{1}{2}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{50} \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2 \mathrm{r}^2}=\mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{50} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{4 \mathrm{r}^2}\right)=\mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{50} \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{(2 \mathrm{r}+1)-(2 \mathrm{r}-1)}{1+(2 \mathrm{r}+1)(2 \mathrm{r}-1)}\right]=\mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{50}\left[\tan ^{-1}(2 \mathrm{r}+1)-\tan ^{-1}(2 \mathrm{r}-1)\right]=\mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}(101)-\tan ^{-1}(1)=\mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{101-1}{1+101}\right)=\mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{100}{102}=\tan \mathrm{p} \\ & \Rightarrow \tan \mathrm{p}=\frac{50}{51}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
(D)
Let $\tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}=\theta \Rightarrow \tan \theta=\frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow \cot \theta=\frac{4}{3}$
$\therefore \operatorname{cosec}^{2} \theta=1+\frac{16}{9}=\frac{25}{9} \Rightarrow \operatorname{cosec} \theta=\frac{5}{3} \Rightarrow \sin \theta=\frac{3}{5}$
$\therefore \sin \left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}\right)=\sin \left(\sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{5}\right)=\frac{3}{5}$
Let $\tan ^{-1} \frac{4}{3}=\phi \Rightarrow \tan \phi=\frac{4}{3} \Rightarrow \cot \phi=\frac{3}{4}$
$\therefore \operatorname{cosec}^{2} \phi=1+\frac{9}{16}=\frac{25}{16} \Rightarrow \operatorname{cosec} \phi=\frac{5}{4} \Rightarrow \sin \phi=\frac{4}{5}$
$\therefore \sin \left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{4}{3}\right)=\sin \left(\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}\right)=\frac{4}{5}$
Hence given expression $=\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)^{2}=\frac{9+16}{25}=1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\tan ^{-1} a+\tan ^{-1} b+\tan ^{-1} c=\pi$, then which of the following is true?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \tan ^{-1} a+\tan ^{-1} b+\tan ^{-1} c=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a+b+c-a b c}{1-a b-b c-c a}\right)=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{a+b+c-a b c}{1-a b-b c-c a}=\tan \pi=0 \\ & \Rightarrow a+b+c=a b c\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned}
& \sin ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1}(1-x)=\cos ^{-1} x \\
& \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1}(1-x)=\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} x \\
& =\frac{\pi}{2}-2 \sin ^{-1} x \\
& {\left[\because \cos ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}\right]} \\
& \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1}(1-x)=\sin ^{-1} 1-\sin ^{-1} 2 x \sqrt{1-x^2} \\
& \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1}(1-x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(1 \sqrt{1-4 x^2\left(1-x^2\right)}-0\right) \\
& \Rightarrow (1-x)=\sqrt{1-4 x^4+4 x^2}=\sqrt{\left(1-2 x^2\right)^2} \\
& \Rightarrow (1-x)=1-2 x^2 \\
& \Rightarrow 2 x^2-x=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x(2 x-1)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x=0, \frac{1}{2} \\
& \therefore x \in\left\{0, \frac{1}{2}\right\}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What is the value of $\sin ^{-1} \frac{12}{13}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{63}{16}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13}\right)+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{63}{16}\right)$
$\begin{array}{r}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13}\right)+\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{63}{16}\right) \\ \left\{\because \sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}\right\} \\ =\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13}\right)-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)+\frac{\pi}{2}+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{63}{\sqrt{(63)^2+(16)^2}}\right) \\ \quad\left\{\because \sin ^{-1} x \pm \sin ^{-1} y=\sin ^{-1}\left(x \sqrt{1-y^2} \pm y \sqrt{\left.1-x^2\right)}\right\}\right. \\ =\sin ^{-1}\left\{\frac{12}{13} \cdot \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)^2}-\frac{4}{5} \cdot \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{12}{13}\right)^2}\right\} \\ +\frac{\pi}{2}+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{63}{65}\right)\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\sin ^{-1}\left\{\frac{12}{13} \cdot \frac{3}{5}-\frac{4}{5} \cdot \frac{5}{13}\right\}+\frac{\pi}{2}+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{63}{65}\right) \\ & =\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{16}{65}\right)+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{63}{65}\right)+\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & =\sin ^{-1}\left\{\frac{16}{65} \cdot \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{63}{65}\right)^2}+\frac{63}{65} \cdot \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{16}{65}\right)^2}\right\}+\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & =\sin ^{-1}(1)+\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}+\frac{\pi}{2}=\pi\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
Given that,
$\begin{aligned}
& \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{x}\right)+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{x}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \therefore \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{x}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{x}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{x}\right)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{x}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{x}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{x^2-16}}{x}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{3}{x}=\frac{\sqrt{x^2-16}}{x} \\
& \Rightarrow 9=x^2-16 \Rightarrow x^2=25 \\
& \Rightarrow x= \pm 5 \\
& \Rightarrow x=5
\end{aligned}$
($\because-5$ is not satisfied the given equation)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \frac{1+\tanh \frac{x}{2}}{1-\tanh \frac{x}{2}} & =\frac{\cosh \frac{x}{2}+\sinh \frac{x}{2}}{\cosh \frac{x}{2}-\sinh \frac{x}{2}} \\ & =\frac{e^{x / 2}}{e^{-x / 2}}=e^x\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
We have given,
As we know, $\sinh ^{-1} x=\log x+\sqrt{1+x^2}$ So, $2 \sin \mathrm{h}^{-1} x=2 \log \left(x+\sqrt{1+x^2}\right)$ $\begin{aligned} & =\log \left(x+\sqrt{1+x^2}\right)^2 \\ & =\log \left[x^2+1+x^2+2 x \sqrt{1+x^2}\right] \\ & 2 \sin \mathrm{h}^{-1} x=\log \left[2 x^2+1+2 x \sqrt{1+x^2}\right] \end{aligned}$ Now, put $x=\frac{a}{\sqrt{1-a^2}}$ $\begin{aligned} & =\log \left[\frac{2 a^2}{1-a^2}+1+\frac{2 a}{\sqrt{1-a^2}} \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-a^2}}\right] \\ & =\log \left[\frac{2 a^2}{1-a^2}+1+\frac{2 a}{1-a^2}\right] \\ & =\log \left[\frac{2 a^2+1-a^2+2 a}{1-a^2}\right] \\ & =\log \left[\frac{a^2+2 a+1}{1-a^2}\right] \\ & \end{aligned}$ Now, by using Eq. (i), $\begin{aligned} & \log \left[\frac{a^2+2 a+1}{1-a^2}\right]=\log \left[\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right] \\ & \frac{a^2+2 a+1}{1-a^2}=\frac{1+x}{1-x} \end{aligned}$ [by using componendo and dividendo rule] $\begin{aligned} & \frac{a^2+2 a+1+1-a^2}{a^2+2 a+1-1+a^2} \\ & =\frac{1+x+1-x}{1+x-1+x} \Rightarrow \frac{2+2 a}{2 a^2+2 a}=\frac{1}{x} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{2(1+a)}{2 a(1+a)}=\frac{1}{x} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{a}=\frac{1}{x} \Rightarrow x=a \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\cos \left(2 \cos ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} x\right)$ at $x=\frac{1}{5}$, where $0 \leq \cos ^{-1} x \leq \pi$ and $-\frac{\pi}{2} \leq \sin ^{-1} x \leq \frac{\pi}{2}$, is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \cos \left(2 \cos ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} x\right)=\cos \left(\cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} x\right) \\ & =\cos \left(\cos ^{-1} x+\frac{\pi}{2}\right) \quad\left[\because \cos ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}\right] \\ & =-\sin \left(\cos ^{-1} x\right) \\ & =-\sin \left(\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-x^2}\right) \\ & =-\sqrt{1-x^2} \\ & =-\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{1}{5}\right)^2} \quad\left[\because x=\frac{1}{5}\right] \\ & =-\frac{2 \sqrt{6}}{5}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
Given that,
$\begin{aligned} & \cot \left(\cos ^{-1} x\right)=\sec \left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{b^2-a^2}}\right)\right\} \\ & \text { Since, } \cos ^{-1} x=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\right) \\ & \quad \text { and } \tan ^{-1} x=\sec ^{-1}\left(\sqrt{1+x^2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \cot \left(\cot ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\right)=\sec \\ & \left\{\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{1+\left(\frac{a}{\sqrt{b^2-a^2}}\right)^2}\right\} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}=\sqrt{\frac{b^2-a^2+a^2}{b^2-a^2}} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}=\frac{b}{\sqrt{b^2-a^2}}\end{aligned}$
On squaring both the sides, we get
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x^2}{1-x^2}=\frac{b^2}{b^2-a^2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x^2 b^2-x^2 a^2=b^2-b^2 x^2 \\
& \quad x^2 b^2+b^2 x^2-x^2 a^2=b^2 \\
& \quad 2 x^2 b^2-x^2 a^2=b^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x^2\left(2 b^2-a^2\right)=b^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{b}{\sqrt{2 b^2-a^2}}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If $\tanh ^{-1} x=a \log \left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right),|x| < 1$ then $a$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\tanh ^{-1} x=a \log \left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right),|x| < 1$ ...(i)
But $\tanh ^{-1} x=\frac{1}{2} \log \left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right)$ ...(ii)
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$a=\frac{1}{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \tan ^{-1} 2+\cot ^{-1}(-3)+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right) \\ & =\tan ^{-1}(2)-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)+\pi-\cot ^{-1}(3)+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right) \\ & =\tan ^{-1}(2)-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)+\pi-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)+\tan ^{-1}(3) \\ & =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2-\frac{1}{2}}{1+2 \cdot \frac{1}{2}}\right)+\pi+\tan ^{-1}(3)-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right) \\ & =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)+\pi+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3-\frac{1}{3}}{1+3 \cdot \frac{1}{3}}\right) \\ & =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)+\pi+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{8}{6}\right) \\ & =\pi+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{3}\right) \\ & =\pi+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)=\pi+\pi / 2 \\ & =3 \pi / 2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & {\left[\sec \left(\tan ^{-1} 2\right)\right]^2+\left[\operatorname{cosec}\left(\cot ^{-1} 3\right)\right]^2} \\ & =\left[\sec \sec ^{-1} \sqrt{5}\right]^2+\left[\operatorname{cosec} \operatorname{cosec}^{-1} \sqrt{10}\right]^2 \\ & =5+10=15\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\sin ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{6}$, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have
$\sin ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{6}$
Let $A=\sin ^{-1} x$
$\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & x=\sin A, \cos A=\sqrt{1-x^2} \\
\text {and } & B=\cos ^{-1} x \\
\Rightarrow & x=\cos B, \sin B=\sqrt{1-x^2}
\end{array}$
Now,
$\begin{aligned}
\sin (A-B) & =\sin A \cos B-\cos A \sin B \\
& =x \cdot x-\sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot \sqrt{1-x^2} \\
& =x^2-1+x^2 \\
\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right) & =2 x^2-1 \\
\Rightarrow 2 x^2-1 & =\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow 2 x^2=\frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow x^2=\frac{3}{4} \\
\Rightarrow \quad x & =\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3} \\ & =\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{2\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}{1-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^2} \\ & =\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{\frac{2}{3}}{\frac{8}{9}} \\ & =\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4} \\ & =\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{4}{5} \quad\left[\because \tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}=\cos ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}\right] \\ & =\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & {\left[\because \sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}\right]} \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+a^2 x^2}-1}{a x}\right)$ and $\left(1+a^2 x^2\right) y^{\prime \prime}+g(x) y^{\prime}=0$ then, the sum of the roots of the equation $1+a^2 x^2+g(x)=0$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+a^2 x^2}-1}{a x}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { put } a x=\tan \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1}(a x) \\ & y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sec \theta-1}{\tan \theta}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-\cos \theta}{\sin \theta}\right)=\tan ^{-1} \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{\theta}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1}(a x) \Rightarrow y^{\prime}=\frac{1}{2} \frac{a}{1+a^2 x^2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(1+a^2 x^2\right) y^{\prime}=\frac{a}{2} \Rightarrow\left(1+a^2 x^2\right) y^{\prime \prime}+2 a^2 x y^{\prime}=0 \\ & \therefore \quad g(x)=2 a^2 x \Rightarrow 1+a^2 x^2+g(x)=0 \\ & a^2 x^2+2 a^2 x+1=0 \\ & \text { Sum of roots }=\frac{-2 a^2}{a^2}=-2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For the least possible value of $n \in \mathbf{Z}$ the solution $(x, y)$ of the equations $\cos ^{-1} x+\left(\sin ^{-1} y\right)^2=\frac{n \pi^2}{4}$ and $\cos ^{-1} x\left(\sin ^{-1} y\right)^2=\frac{\pi^4}{16}$, is
questions.solution:
$\left(\sin ^{-1} y\right)^2+\cos ^{-1} x=\frac{n \pi^2}{4}$
and $\left(\cos ^{-1} x\right)\left(\sin ^{-1} y\right)^2=\frac{\pi^4}{16}$
Let $\cos ^{-1} x=a$ and $\sin ^{-1} y=b \Rightarrow a+b^2=\frac{n \pi^2}{4} \ldots(i)$
$a \cdot b^2=\frac{\pi^4}{16} \ldots(ii)$
$\therefore \quad a-b=\sqrt{\left(a+b^2\right)^2-4 a b}=\sqrt{\frac{n^2 \pi^4}{16}-\frac{4 \pi^4}{16}}$
Now, least value of $n$ can be 2
$a-b^2=\sqrt{\frac{4 \pi^4}{16}-\frac{4 \pi^4}{16}} \Rightarrow a-b^2=0 \ldots(iii)$
Adding Eqs. (i) and (iii), we get
$2 a=\frac{n \pi^2}{4} \text { or } 2 a=\frac{2 \pi^2}{4} \quad(\because n=2)$
or $\quad a=\frac{\pi^2}{4} \Rightarrow \cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi^2}{4} \Rightarrow$ $x=\cos \left(\frac{\pi^2}{4}\right)$
Now, from Eq. (iii), $a=b$
$\left(\sin ^{-1} y\right)^2=\frac{\pi^2}{4} \text { or } \sin ^{-1} y= \pm \frac{\pi}{2}$
or $\quad y=\sin \left( \pm \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ or $y= \pm 1$
Therefore, ordered pair is $\left(\cos \frac{\pi^2}{4}, \pm 1\right)$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
Given equation is
$\begin{gathered}
\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^2+\left(\cot ^{-1} x\right)^2=\frac{5 \pi^2}{8} \\
\left(\tan ^{-1} x+\cot ^{-1} x\right)^2-2 \tan ^{-1} x \cdot \cot ^{-1} x=\frac{5 x^2}{8} \\
{\left[\because \tan ^{-1} x+\cot ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}\right]}
\end{gathered}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{\pi^2}{4}-2 \tan ^{-1} x\left(\pi / 2-\tan ^{-1} x\right)=\frac{5 \pi^2}{8} \\ & \Rightarrow-2 \cdot \frac{\pi \tan ^{-1} x}{2}+2\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^2=\frac{5 \pi^2}{8}-\frac{\pi^2}{4} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)^2-\frac{\pi \tan ^{-1} x}{2}=\frac{3 \pi^2}{16}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{cc}\therefore \quad & \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\frac{\pi}{2} \pm \sqrt{\frac{\pi^2}{4}+\frac{3 \pi^2}{4}}}{2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\frac{\pi}{2} \pm \pi}{2} \\ & =\frac{3 \pi}{4},-\frac{\pi}{4}\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & x=\tan \left(\frac{3 \pi}{4}\right), \tan \left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ \Rightarrow & x=-1,-1\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The set of values of $\alpha$ such that $f: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ defined by $f(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(x^2+x+\alpha^2\right)$ is onto is
questions.solution:
Let $A=\left\{x: 0 \leq x < \frac{\pi}{2}\right\}$
Since $f: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow A$ is an onto function, therefore,
Range of $f=A$
$\Rightarrow 0 \leq f(x) \leq \frac{\pi}{2}$
for all $x \in \mathbf{R}$
$\Rightarrow 0 \leq \tan ^{-1}\left(x^2+x+\alpha^2\right) \leq \frac{\pi}{2}$
for all $x \in \mathbf{R}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 0 \leq x^2+x+\alpha^2 \leq \infty \quad$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}$
$\Rightarrow \quad x^2+x+\alpha^2 \geq 0 \quad$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 1-4 \alpha^2 \leq 0 \Rightarrow \alpha^2 \geq \frac{1}{4}$
$\therefore \quad\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{2}\right) \cup\left(\frac{1}{2}, \infty\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{array}{r}
\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{5}+\frac{1}{2} \sec ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{8}=\frac{\pi}{8} \\
\Rightarrow \quad\left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{5}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{8}\right)+\frac{1}{2} \sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{8}
\end{array}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{1}{5}+\frac{1}{8}}{1-\frac{1}{5} \times \frac{1}{8}}\right]+\frac{1}{2} \sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{8}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \tan ^{-1} \frac{13}{39}+\frac{1}{2} \sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{8} \\ \Rightarrow & 2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{13}{39}+\sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ \Rightarrow & 2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3}+\sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ \Rightarrow & \tan ^{-1} \frac{\frac{2}{3}}{1-\frac{1}{9}}+\sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ \Rightarrow & \tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}+\sec ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{4}\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{rr}\Rightarrow \quad & \tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}+\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x^2-1}=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{3}{4}+\sqrt{x^2-1}}{1-\frac{3}{4} \sqrt{x^2-1}}\right]=\frac{\pi}{4}\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{cc}\Rightarrow & \frac{3+4 \sqrt{x^2-1}}{4-3 \sqrt{x^2-1}}=1 \\ \Rightarrow & 3+4 \sqrt{x^2-1}=4-3 \sqrt{x^2-1} \Rightarrow 7 \sqrt{x^2-1}=1 \\ \Rightarrow & \sqrt{x^2-1}=\frac{1}{7} \Rightarrow x^2-1=\frac{1}{49} \Rightarrow x^2=\frac{50}{49}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
